CONTENT.Early life: Bafodé Diakité was born on 6 January 2001 in Toulouse, France to Guinean parents. He holds French and Guinean nationalities.
CONTENT.Club career: Diakité made his professional debut for Toulouse in a 1–0 Ligue 1 win over Reims on 5 December 2018, at the age of 17.On 5 August 2022, Lille announced the signing of Diakité on a four-year deal.
CONTENT.External links: Bafodé Diakité – French league stats at Ligue 1 – also available in FrenchBafodé Diakité at SoccerwayTFC ProfileBafodé Diakité at the French Football Federation (in French)Bafodé Diakité at the French Football Federation (archived) (in French)
CONTENT.International career: Diakité is a youth international for France. His club, Lille, denied him permission along with his teammates Lucas Chevalier and Leny Yoro to compete in the 2024 Summer Olympics on home soil.

SUMMARY: Bafodé Diakité (born 6 January 2001) is a French professional footballer who plays as a defender for Ligue 1 club Lille.
